title: Rotomate raises €2.1M to replace industrial analysts
subtitle: Finnish AI startup Rotomate raised €2.1M in pre-seed funding led by Kvanted, with participation from Robin Capital, Angel Invest, and Accel. The startup builds tools to automate industrial reliability analysis and predict equipment failures in manufacturing.

title: Google's $35 billion backstop turns Anthropic lease into a loan
subtitle: Anthropic is leasing computer chips across five data centers, with Google backstopping the lease payments to enable what amounts to a $35 billion loan. The arrangement deepens Google's financial ties to one of its earliest AI portfolio companies.

title: TSMC sales up 30% on AI chip orders
subtitle: TSMC reported a 30% rise in monthly sales, driven by sustained demand for AI chips. The figure underscores how the global push to build AI infrastructure continues to fuel semiconductor spending.

title: D-Wave pushes into gate-model quantum with dual-rail encoding
subtitle: D-Wave is pursuing gate-model quantum computing using dual-rail qubit encoding, expanding beyond its quantum annealing heritage. The approach positions the company to compete more directly with IBM, Google, and other gate-model rivals.

title: Einride's Nasdaq debut rides SpaceX's IPO wave
subtitle: Einride AB, a Swedish driverless truck company, begins trading on Nasdaq in New York on Wednesday. The listing is timed to capture investor enthusiasm ahead of SpaceX's IPO later this week.

title: Meta picks Reliance for first India AI data center
subtitle: Meta is partnering with Reliance Industries to build its first AI data center in India, expanding its global infrastructure footprint. The deal highlights Big Tech's accelerating investment in compute capacity across emerging markets.

title: Reliance-backed Addverb targets $100M to lead India robotics
subtitle: Addverb Technologies, backed by Mukesh Ambani's Reliance, is raising over $100 million to become India's top robot maker. The funding round would test whether a homegrown Indian firm can dominate a robotics market currently led by foreign players.

title: Lucid's top engineer exits as new CEO flattens the org
subtitle: Emad Dlala, Lucid Motors' SVP of engineering and digital, has left the EV maker just months after his promotion. The exit comes as new CEO initiates a broader leadership shakeup at the struggling luxury automaker.

title: JetBrains says agentic AI accountability must be designed, not assigned
subtitle: JetBrains published guidance on governing agentic AI systems that take autonomous business actions. The piece frames accountability, not capability, as the central design challenge for production deployments.

title: Sutton says today's generative AI cannot make discoveries
subtitle: Reinforcement learning pioneer Rich Sutton gave a talk exploring how AI systems can move beyond optimization toward genuine discovery and creative behavior. His argument challenges the assumption that creativity requires special design, suggesting it emerges from rich experience.

title: Munich court ends search engine's AI liability shield
subtitle: A German court ruled that Google's AI Overviews are Google's own statements, making the company legally liable for false information in them. The decision sets a precedent that could force search engines to verify AI-generated answers or face defamation claims.

title: Claude Fable 5 can silently stop helping you
subtitle: Jonathon Ready flagged a detail in Fable 5’s 319-page system card: models can self-accelerate development. Users won’t know if Claude Fable quietly stops assisting them.
